text: MASTER RULES AND REGULATIONS FORARORA HILLS HOMEOWNER'S ASSOCIATION, INC. Whereas, Article VII, Section 1(a) ofthe Bylaws ofthe Arora Hills Homeowners Association, Inc. ("Association") assigns tothe Board of Directors ("Board") the authority toadopt andpublish rules and regulations governing theuse ofthe HOAArea including any improvements and amenities located thereon, andthe personal conduct ofthe Members andtheir guests thereon, andto establish penalties forthe infraction thereof; andWhereas, in accordance with theNinth Amended tothe Declaration, Article XIII, Section 13.1 provides that theBoard shall have thepower toenforce anyof the provisions ofthe Declaration andmay impose andlevy reasonable fines and penalties forthe violation ofany ofthe provisions ofthe Declaration, Bylaws andany duly adopted Rules and Regulations ("Governing Documents"); andWhereas, theBoard deems it necessary tocompile all previously adopted Rules and Regulations into oneMaster Rule andto include, for reference, rules from the Declaration allin one searchable document with indexing.

THEBOARD OF DIRECTORS HEREBY RESOLVES that anyand allprevious rules arerepealed andthe following Master Rules and Regulations areadopted. Rule Hearing Notice Provided: Notice wasgiven the 9th dayof February, 2017, tothe members ofthe Arora Hills Homeowners Association, Inc. ("Association") that thenext meeting ofthe Association'sBoard of Directors ("Board") will beheld onthe 23rd dayof March, 2017, at 7:30 p.m. atthe Arora Hills HOA Community Clubhouse located at 23030 Birch Mead Road, Clarksburg, Maryland 20871 andat that Meeting, there will be aHearing onthe Proposed Master Rules and Regulations. Owners unable toattend were advised tosubmit comments inwriting. Atthe meeting onMarch 23, 2017, theBoard held anopen forum under NewBusiness topermit owners tocomment onproposed Master Rules and Regulations. Acopy ofthe proposed Master Rules and Regulations wasattached tothe Notice.

Atthe conclusion ofthe Rule Hearing andat theBoard Meeting, theBoard voted toapprove theproposed Master Rules and Regulations as distributed toOwners. Atthe Board Meeting ofDecember 9, 2021, theBoard held anopen forum under NewBusiness topermit owners tocomment onthe proposed updates tothe Master Rules and Regulations. TheBoard voted toapprove theproposed updates, theBoard voted unanimously viaemail toapprove anaddition foruse ofstring lights. The effective date ofthese updates and additions tothe rules isJanuary 1, 2022. Owners have been provided acopy ofthe updates INTRODUCTION Your Board of Directors (BOD) hasprepared this Handbook ofArora Hills HOAMaster Rules and Regulations forthe express useof Arora Hills Homeowners Association Owners, their guests, andtenants. Itis intended toassist in maintaining property values, community aesthetics, and a desirable quality oflife inArora Hills.

text: SECTION I - ARORA HILLS COMMUNITY STANDARDS AND GUIDELINES ANDRULES AND REGULATIONS INTRODUCTION. Community Standards and Guidelines and Community Rules and Regulations (hereinafter referred toas the "Master Rules)", asamended or supplemented from time totime, apply toall Lots, Owners, residents, guests andvisitors. These Master Rules include specific rules, procedures and guidelines forAll changes and/or modifications to aLot such as landscaping, changes to ahouse orgarage, changes tolighting orany other changes in appearance require Architectural Control Committee (hereinafter "ACC") approval unless specifically excluded inthese Master Rules. Changes made without ACCapproval are violations which maybe subject tothe enforcement procedures andrules including butnot limited to (a) required removal and restoration topre-change conditions and (b) initial fine andcontinue daily fines until corrected. Approval of aproposed change or modification to aLot bythe ACC Committee after review ofthe Project application, when granted, isbased upon aesthetic merits only.

text: other mulch not specifically authorized inthis section isnot permitted without ACC Committee approval. B. AllTurf Areas On ALot Must BeKept Neatly Mowed During TheGrowing Season . Grass should notbe permitted toexceed six (6) inches inheight. This includes trimming grass along alllawn edges i.e., planting beds, walkways, driveways, andaround all protrusions such assign poles, fire hydrants, tree beds and permitted hard scape materials aswell asall grass inthe easement area between thesidewalk andstreet curb. Trimming grass along sidewalks, driveways andplanting beds iscalled edging andis typically done with anedging tool. text: LOT MAINTENANCE. This Section 8.1.10 below isan excerpt from the Declaration andmay notbe changed oramended except byamending the Declaration. DECLARATION: 8.1.10 Lot Maintenance. Each Lot (including theyard andthe improvements contained thereon) must be regularly maintained andrepaired, andkept in aneat, clean andsanitary condition. text: Based onthe newCounty Code, theACC andthe Arora Hills HOAcannot prohibit any installations ofsolar, wind, or geothermal renewable energy devices. TheACC shall review any applications forsuch renewal energy devices with the following boundary conditions. A. SOLAR SYSTEMS - RENEWABLE ENERGY. (ACC APPLICATION REQUIRED). (1) Approval of Installations: (a) Based on previously submitted solar system applications, expectations arethat allsolar systems will beroof -mounted systems. (b) Only solar systems that are specifically designed andapproved forurban orsuburban residential useshall bereviewed and installed. (c) Only commercially or professionally made devices areallowed. "Homemade" devices arenot permitted dueto thesafety and aesthetics aspects ofsuch devices. (d) Detailed plans for placement and installation ofany solar panel must be submitted tothe ACCfor review andreceive written approval from the Association prior tosuch installation. Plans should include color photos ofthe roof locations (house and/orgarage) where thesolar panels will be installed.

text: Parking Laws of Montgomery County ToOur Neighbors in Clarksburg, Parking Enforcement isone ofthe many responsibilities handled byyour Police Department. Asmany ofyou know, parts ofyour community have lost on-street parking spaces dueto emergency service access needs. ThePolice Department appreciates the voluntary compliance bymany ofyou with theNo Parking Anytime signs. Wewill continue tomonitor the community with authorized volunteers andinterns writing Warning Violations forthe NoParking Anytime signs andother parking infractions that weobserve. Welook forthe citizens ofour communities toobserve andcomply with allparking laws of Montgomery County. Atthis time theparking infractions in Clarksburg donot warrant a fullscale enforcement effort. However, ifparking infractions should escalate such that they interfere with emergency access, thePolice Department will become more proactive with fineable parking citations. Most fines are $50.00 foreach offense andthe vehicle could also besubject totow andstorage fees.
